arts.ttl: Dreesen and Bayern happy with centre-back pairing
arts.sum: Speaking after Bayern München's 1-1 draw with Bayer Leverkusen at the weekend, the club's CEO, Jan-Christian Dreesen outlined how happy he is with the current pair of central defenders in Vincent Kompany's starting XI.

arts.ttl: How much Aston Villa are charging for Champions League tickets and how it compares to rivals
arts.sum: The most expensive Liverpool ticket for a Champions League fixture is £61, although the same ticket is significantly cheaper for Over-65 (£45.75), Young Adult (£30.50) and Junior (£9). Villa confirmed the ticket prices for the respective fixtures in September, which has led to comparisons being made with their rival British clubs.

arts.ttl: What Aston Villa stand to make as Champions League returns to Villa Park
arts.sum: This coming campaign in the Champions League where the Villans will take on Bayern Munich, Bologna, Juventus, and Celtic at Villa Park, has seen the club raise matchday ticket prices by as much as 55%, with tickets to cost fans between £70 and £97 for a one-off game. Not since a quarter-final exit in the 1982/83 competition have Villa hosted a game in the top-tier knockout club competition on the continent, and Wednesday evening’s clash, despite the understandable anger over the significant hiking of ticket prices, will be a sell-out affair.

arts.ttl: Youth League: Aston Villa 0-1 Bayern Munich
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. FC Bayern München - Wed, Oct 2nd 2024, 13:00 Aston Villa suffered a narrow defeat at the hands of Bayern Munich in the UEFA Youth League at Bodymoor Heath. The Young Lions were more than a match for their German opponents throughout a hard-fought contest, with chances at a premium in a goalless first half. Triston Rowe and Mason Cotcher came close to opening the scoring as the hosts made a strong start to the second half before Bayern edged ahead through Jonah Kusi-Asare on 64 minutes. Villa poured forward in search of a leveller and agonsingly saw Thierry Katsukunya glance a header inches wide in stoppage time as an equaliser eluded them. Josep Gombau’s team began their campaign with a defeat against Young Boys while Munich’s youngsters had overcome Dinamo Zagreb 2-1. The visitors, who reached the quarter-finals last season, started brightly and forced Sam Proctor into a pair of fine saves with six minutes on the clock. Travis Patterson and Ben Broggio were causing Bayern problems down Villa’s left-hand side, but the hosts were struggling to open their opponents up despite bossing possession. A cagey game burst into life just past the half-hour mark when Proctor denied Michael Scott and Villa swept forward at great pace, working the ball into the box and seeing a Broggio shot blocked by David Daiber. The lively Scott came close to capping a fine solo run, in which he left three defenders in his wake, but slid his effort just wide of Proctor’s far post. Villa flew out of the blocks at the start of the second half, pressing high and creating a chance for Cotcher, who poked wide under pressure from a Bayern defender. Moments later Rowe burst into the box from right-back and smashed an effort that was palmed over by Leon Klanac as the home side piled on the pressure. After clinging on in the opening stages of the half, the German side composed themselves and hit the front via a flowing counter-attack after 64 minutes. Captain Adam Aznou curled a pass behind the Villa backline and into the path of Kusi-Asare, who placed a first-time shot past Proctor. Bradley Burrowes and Omar Khedr were summoned from the bench to add fresh legs and creativity to Villa’s attacking play, but the final ball was missing at the end of several good openings. As the game moved into the final ten minutes, Broggio bore down on goal but saw his shot blocked by a superb last-ditch slide tackle from Aznou. Rowe sent a shot narrowly wide before Jimoh-Aloba went even closer following a mazy solo run, blasting a shot that was tipped over by Klanac. Katsukunya glanced a header inches wide in stoppage time as Villa pushed without reward for an equaliser. Aston Villa: Proctor, Patterson, Routh, Katsukunya, Rowe, Borland, Hemmings, Jimoh-Aloba, Pavey (Khedr 70’), Broggio, Cotcher (Burrowes 70’). Unused subs: Hammond, Fortes, Simpson, Mulley, Carroll, Bloomfield, Lynskey. Upcoming Fixtures Aston Villa U21 Vs. Newcastle United U21 - Mon, Dec 12th 2024, 19:00 Middlesbrough U21 Vs. Aston Villa U21 - Sun, Dec 12th 2024, 14:00 Leicester City U21 Vs. Aston Villa U21 - Fri, Jan 1st 2025, 19:00

arts.ttl: Aston Villa warned not to follow example of Premier League rival
arts.sum: Aston Villa face Bayern Munich in their latest Champions League game with words of warning over what they should priorities to become an established elite clubs, from former defender Richard Dunne

arts.ttl: Gary Shaw cause of death as Aston Villa pay tribute to hero vs Bayern Munich in Champions League
arts.sum: A club statement upon his passing read: “Aston Villa Football Club is deeply shocked and profoundly saddened to learn that Gary Shaw, one of our European Cup-winning heroes, has passed away. Aston Villa have honoured late club hero Gary Shaw ahead of Wednesday’s Champions League match at Villa Park, their first home tie in Europe’s elite competition for 41 years.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa prepare for huge night six years on from cabbage-gate
arts.sum: Aston Villa are preparing to face Bayern Munich in the Champions League this evening in the club’s first home fixture in this competition since the early eighties. Unai Emery’s transformation of the club has seen Villa rise from the lower regions of the Premier League to Europe’s biggest stage and the visit of Bayern Munich […] The post Aston Villa prepare for huge night six years on from cabbage-gate first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: Kompany thrives under high expectations at Bayern
arts.sum: Vincent Kompany says he relishes the high expectations placed on him at Bayern Munich.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich’s Jamal Musiala “not planning on move” to the Premier League amid interest
arts.sum: “I don’t try to think too far forward about where I want to be because situations can always change by the year or by the month. For Bayern Munich superstar Jamal Musiala, PL clubs are making a beeline for him since becoming an undisputed starter for the German giants.

arts.ttl: W*nk and you missed it: Bayern Munich’s Sven Ulreich forgiven by Bayer Leverkusen’s Simon Rolfes, but will the DFB drop its probe?
arts.sum: Speaking to DAZN (as captured by @iMiaSanMia ), Bayer Leverkusen sporting director Simon Rolfes said that Sven Ulreich contacted him, apologized, and that the matter is over in his eyes. Will the organization look to crackdown on future offenders by making an example of Ulreich?
